findings.signature: db.exec('BEGIN IMMEDIATE') / db.exec('COMMIT') / db.exec('ROLLBACK') — manual transaction control
findings.notes: Manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT/ROLLBACK bypasses better-sqlite3 transaction wrapper; Turso lacks exec()-based transaction control and requires different TX API

findings.signature: db.prepare('PRAGMA table_info(session_state)')
findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info used for migration column detection; Turso may require different schema introspection API

findings.signature: db.prepare('SELECT 1 FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = ? AND name = ?')
findings.notes: sqlite_master introspection; Turso supports sqlite_master but pattern should be verified

findings.signature: db.exec() for CREATE TABLE + ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN migrations
findings.notes: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN migration pattern; Turso supports this but multi-statement exec() may need adaptation

findings.signature: db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON')
findings.notes: Triple pragma initialization; Turso has no .pragma() API — journal_mode, busy_timeout, foreign_keys must be set via connection string or SQL PRAGMA statements

findings.signature: db.transaction((rows) => { for (turn of rows) insertTurn.run(...) }) — parameterized transaction wrapper
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 db.transaction() synchronous wrapper; Turso transaction API is async and has different semantics

findings.signature: CREATE TABLE analytics_turns (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, ... UNIQUE(claude_session_id, transcript_path, byte_start))
findings.notes: AUTOINCREMENT keyword; Turso/libSQL supports it but behavior may differ with distributed replication

findings.signature: INSERT INTO embedding_cache (...) ON CONFLICT(...) DO UPDATE SET embedding = excluded.embedding, last_used_at = datetime('now')
findings.notes: Upsert with datetime('now') function; Turso supports ON CONFLICT but datetime() function behavior should be verified

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE RENAME TO + CREATE TABLE + INSERT OR REPLACE + DROP TABLE — multi-step schema migration
findings.notes: Complex migration with RENAME/INSERT/DROP in sequence; Turso schema migration may need different approach for distributed state

findings.signature: db.transaction((key) => { DELETE FROM ... WHERE EXISTS (...); UPDATE ... SET profile_key = ? WHERE profile_key = '' })
findings.notes: Transaction with correlated subquery DELETE + UPDATE; Turso supports but performance characteristics differ

findings.signature: db.pragma('shrink_memory')
findings.notes: PRAGMA shrink_memory is better-sqlite3 specific; Turso has no .pragma() API and this memory management directive does not exist in standard SQLite

findings.signature: PRAGMA table_info(tableName) with optional ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA prefix
findings.notes: PRAGMA table_info with schema-qualified form; Turso .pragma() absent, needs SQL EXEC adaptation

findings.signature: db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() — shard/schema detection
findings.notes: PRAGMA database_list for ATTACH detection; Turso may not expose attached databases the same way

findings.signature: db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON')
findings.notes: Same triple-pragma pattern as session-analytics-db; Turso .pragma() API absent — must be set via connection options or SQL

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM entity_catalog; for (...) INSERT INTO entity_catalog (...) })
findings.notes: Full table rebuild inside transaction; Turso transaction API differs (async), DELETE-then-INSERT pattern safe but must wrap correctly

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { for (entity of entities) stmt.run(memoryId, ...) }) — INSERT OR REPLACE loop
findings.notes: Synchronous transaction wrapper with INSERT OR REPLACE; Turso requires async transaction API

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status='success'; DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid=?; INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?) })
findings.notes: Cross-table transaction touching memory_index + vec_memories with BigInt rowid; Turso vec_memories (sqlite-vec virtual table) likely absent, requires vector index replacement

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status='retry' WHERE id=? AND embedding_status='pending' AND retry_count=? AND last_retry_at=? — optimistic concurrency
findings.notes: Optimistic concurrency via retry_count+last_retry_at CAS; works in Turso but distributed replication may increase CAS conflicts

findings.signature: SELECT id FROM memory_index ... LIMIT -1 OFFSET ? — SQLite-specific negative LIMIT
findings.notes: LIMIT -1 OFFSET N is SQLite-specific pagination idiom; libSQL may handle differently, needs verification

findings.signature: BigInt(id) for vec_memories rowid insertion
findings.notes: BigInt rowid for sqlite-vec virtual table INSERT; Turso lacks sqlite-vec loadExtension, entire vec_memories table unavailable
